title: Rei II vs Armisael Set up for failure from the start
display_title: Rei II vs Armisael Set up for failure from the start
wikitext:
[]: Rei II vs. Armisael: Set up for failure from the start             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Mon Apr 14, 2014 2:41 pm It's obvious that in terms of the plot, the Armisael fight served two purposes: to remove Unit 00 from operational use (as having an extra Evangelion available complicates the Kaworu fight and EoE), and to set up the Reiquarium reveal in the next episode. But let's look at how obvious this is before any of the above is even KNOWN: * Unit 00 is sortied with a sniper rifle, described as its choice armament...against a (relatively) paper-thin opponent at POINT-BLANK RANGE - a shotgun would have seemed far more effective, barring the ineffectiveness of non-Deus Ex Machinas against the Angels. While we're at it, most engagements with the Angels occur at shotgun range, with the exception of Sahaquiel (range and size limitations), Iruel (shotgunning the MAGI seems like a bad idea), Leliel (only killable from the inside), and Arael (range limitations) - why no shotgun-type weapon was ever designed or equipped is never explained, despite it being ideal for large-scale urban combat. * Unit 02 was also sortied, but given Asuka's mental condition, it was known that she would be of no help to Rei in-combat. * I do not know if Units 01 and/or 02 were equipped with the same self-destruct failsafe that 00 was given, but if not, it further pushes the idea that Rei II's usefulness, as far as NERV and SEELE were concerned, extended to the Armisael fight. Now, why exactly did Rei II NEED to be disposed of? Was there a greater chance that she wouldn't cooperate with Instrumentality plans after her most recent experiences with the other pilots, which Rei III does not have? Or was it a happy accident that benefited the above parties the most?

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Apr 14, 2014 2:52 pm I think it was less disposal of Rei, and more an acknowledgement that...Rei was disposable. She could be replaced as many times as necessary until the Scenario. Many times, it seemed they simply had no idea what the Angels would do, so they poked them with giant, mega-calibered sticks to see what would happen. In that sense, a sniper rifle makes sense: try to piss it off at range and see where it goes from there. I think it was the perfect storm of bad events that combined to kill Rei II.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 14, 2014 4:00 pm I think calling this a deliberate act is overthinking it - NERV was simply out of resources at this point. Even before the battle, there's this huge montage about how everybody is depressed and worn-out, from Misato, to Asuka, to Ritsuko.... It's not that they didn't want to send out Rei on better conditions, it's that they couldn't.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Apr 14, 2014 4:53 pm Uh... No. First off, the reiquarium was revealed in the very same episode, #23. Rei was NOT at point-blank range but was at some distance trying to keep an eye on it. It just moved really fast when it wanted to. Asuka's complete inability to get the eva to respond was a surprise, so that wasn't a set-up either. Nerv in no way WANTED Rei to fail... in fact Gendo very clearly was not happy with this outcome. They don't use shotguns because shotguns would be useless at all but point-blank range or unless using slugs. Killing angels means delivering devastating force to specific locations (the core), not peppering them with mass unfocused trauma that they would generally ignore or regenerate immediately even if you did get past the ATF with such a weapon. The pallette riffles are also generally useless but at least they have greater accuracy and ability to hit the same location repeatedly at range. And what reason do you have to doubt that every eva has a self-destruct? Just because they don't all use them doesn't mean they don't exist. I could perhaps see Gendo not putting one in 01 or making it inopperative, but 02 would definately have one; it's not like Nerv actually gives a hint of a shit about Asuka except as cannon fodder anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 14, 2014 5:24 pm In Rebuild, at least, the self-destruct seems to be a standard feature - both Mari and Asuka use one at some point.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Apr 14, 2014 5:27 pm Considering what the Evangelion are supposed to be, doesn't it just make sense they have a way to kill them if necessary?
